name: La Lumiere School
about: La Lumiere School is an independent, co-educational college-preparatory boarding and day school in La Porte, Indiana, founded in 1963 on a 190-acre wooded campus. Originally established as a Catholic day school for boys, it now enrolls roughly 190 students in grades 9-12 plus a postgraduate year. Its athletic teams, the Lakers, are nationally known in basketball, and notable alumni include U.S. Chief Justice John Roberts and comedian Jim Gaffigan.
college_matriculation: Graduates attend a range of four-year colleges; the school emphasizes college-preparatory outcomes.
accreditation: ISACS (Independent Schools Association of the Central States)
notable_alumni: John Roberts (U.S. Chief Justice), Jim Gaffigan, Jaren Jackson Jr., Jordan Poole, Jaden Ivey
financial_aid_notes: Need-based aid and merit scholarships offered.

name: Maharishi School of the Age of Enlightenment
about: Maharishi School (Maharishi School of the Age of Enlightenment) is an independent, non-denominational college-preparatory school in Fairfield, Iowa, founded in 1974 on a 10-acre campus within Maharishi International University. It is known for its Consciousness-Based education, with students and teachers practicing Transcendental Meditation twice daily. The school enrolls a global community of students from more than 30 countries and offers a small boarding program for grades 9-12.
accreditation: ISACS (Independent Schools Association of the Central States)
notable_alumni: Ben Foster (actor), Claire Hoffman (journalist), Eli Lieb (singer-songwriter)
financial_aid_notes: Domestic boarding students may receive up to 40% off total tuition, room, and board.

name: Scattergood Friends School
about: Scattergood Friends School is a small Quaker coeducational boarding and day school in West Branch, Iowa, founded in 1890. It offers a progressive, student-centered college-preparatory program grounded in Quaker values of simplicity, peace, integrity, community, equality, and stewardship, with a working farm and shared-work program on its rural campus. Roughly 45 students attend, about 71% of high schoolers boarding, and acceptance to a four-year college is a graduation requirement.
college_matriculation: Recent placements include Reed, Macalester, Oberlin, Cornell, Wake Forest, Howard, Earlham, Syracuse, University of Iowa, Iowa State, and University of Wisconsin.
accreditation: ISACS (Independent Schools Association of the Central States)
financial_aid_notes: Sliding-scale tuition and merit scholarships available.

name: Maur Hill Mount Academy
about: Maur Hill - Mount Academy is a coeducational Catholic, college-preparatory boarding and day school in Atchison, Kansas, sponsored by St. Benedict's Abbey and Mount St. Scholastica. It was formed in 2003 from the merger of Maur Hill Prep (founded 1919) and Mount St. Scholastica Academy (founded 1863). Roughly 185 students in grades 9-12 attend, with about 45% boarding and a notable international enrollment.
college_matriculation: About 98% of graduates continue to college.
accreditation: Cognia (formerly North Central Association); NCEA member
financial_aid_notes: About 54% of students receive aid; average grant around $8,000; merit scholarships offered.

name: St. John's Military School
about: St. John's Military School was an Episcopal all-boys boarding military school in Salina, Kansas, founded in 1887 by Bishop Elisha Thomas. It served male cadets in grades 6-12 for 131 years. The school CLOSED after the 2018-2019 academic year amid declining enrollment and abuse allegations, and no longer enrolls students; the figures here are historical.
accreditation: AdvancED; Kansas State Department of Education (historical; school closed 2019)
notable_alumni: Nicholas Dockery (Medal of Honor recipient), Dean Hargrove (television producer), Mark Cerney

name: Thomas More Prep-Marian
about: Thomas More Prep-Marian is a coeducational Catholic college-preparatory junior/senior high school in Hays, Kansas, with roots tracing to Hays Catholic College (1908) and formed in its current configuration in 1981 from the merger of local Catholic schools. Affiliated with the Capuchin Franciscan order, it serves about 280 students in grades 6-12 and accepts a small number of boarding and international students. Its teams compete as the Monarchs.
notable_alumni: Jeff Colyer (Governor of Kansas), John L. Allen Jr. (journalist), David J. Tholen (astronomer)
financial_aid_notes: Heartland Parish stewardship families pay no tuition; base school fees apply to all families.

name: Oneida Baptist Institute
about: Oneida Baptist Institute is a coeducational Southern Baptist boarding school in Oneida, Clay County, Kentucky, founded in 1899 by James Anderson Burns to help end local feuding. Affiliated with the Kentucky Baptist Convention, it serves day students K-12 and boarding students in grades 9-12, with roughly 275-300 students drawn from Kentucky, other states, and about 20 countries. Notable alumni include Nvidia co-founder Jensen Huang and former Kentucky Governor Bert Combs.
notable_alumni: Jensen Huang (Nvidia co-founder), Bert Combs (Governor of Kentucky), Russell Coleman (Kentucky Attorney General)
financial_aid_notes: Low tuition; significant institutional subsidy of boarding costs.

name: Academy of the Sacred Heart
affiliation: Roman Catholic (Society of the Sacred Heart)
about: The Academy of the Sacred Heart is an independent Catholic girls' school in Grand Coteau, Louisiana, founded in 1821 under St. Philippine Duchesne and run by the Society of the Sacred Heart. Set on a 250-acre campus listed on the National Register of Historic Places, it enrolls girls from PK3 through grade 12. The school closed its residential boarding program for girls in 2025, so boarding-specific figures no longer apply.
religious_affiliation: Roman Catholic (Society of the Sacred Heart)
accreditation: ISAS (Independent Schools Association of the Southwest); Cognia/AdvancED
